property.name: Occidental Paradise Dambulla
property.tagline: At the heart of Sri Lanka's cultural triangle
property.address: Kubukkadanwala, Dambulla 21100, Central Province, Sri Lanka

items.name: Room Makeup
items.description: Full room cleaning and bed making.

items.name: Airport Transfer (BIA)
items.description: Chauffeur-driven transfer to/from Bandaranaike International Airport via Colombo.

items.name: Sigiriya Day Transfer
items.description: Return transfer to Sigiriya Rock Fortress (20 min drive) with waiting time.

items.name: Minneriya / Kaudulla Safari Transfer
items.description: Return transfer to Minneriya or Kaudulla National Park for elephant gathering safaris.

items.name: Attraction Ticket Booking
items.description: We arrange tickets for Sigiriya, Dambulla Cave Temple, Polonnaruwa and more.

restaurants.name: Paddy Field Restaurant
restaurants.description: Inspired by the lush paddy fields of the Cultural Triangle. Authentic Sri Lankan rice & curry, clay pot cooking, and traditional sweets.

items.name: Ela Hodhi (Leafy Green Curry)
items.description: Traditional greens simmered in coconut milk with turmeric and garlic.

items.name: Isso Wade (Prawn Fritters)
items.description: Crispy lentil fritters topped with whole prawns.

items.name: Traditional Rice & Curry (Veg)
items.description: 8 vegetable curries, dhal, papadam, pol sambol.

items.name: Crab Curry
items.description: Blue swimmer crab in a rich roasted coconut milk gravy.

items.name: Lamb Kiri Hodi
items.description: Slow-braised lamb in white coconut curry, string hoppers.

items.name: Chicken Ambul Thiyal
items.description: Sour black pepper chicken cooked dry in a clay pot — a signature of southern Sri Lanka.

bars.name: The Rock Bar
bars.description: Poolside bar with views towards the famous Dambulla rock fortress. Arrack cocktails and cold beers.

treatments.name: Traditional Thai Massage
treatments.description: A full-body stretching and pressure-point massage performed on a floor mat using traditional Thai techniques. Excellent for releasing tension after a day of climbing Sigiriya or trekking temple sites.
treatments.cancellationPolicy: Cancel at least 2 hours in advance.

treatments.name: Ayurvedic Herbal Oil Massage
treatments.description: A deeply therapeutic massage using warm medicated herbal oils selected to your body type (dosha). Rooted in the 3,000-year-old Sri Lankan Ayurvedic tradition.
treatments.cancellationPolicy: Cancel at least 2 hours in advance.

treatments.name: Heritage Couples Package
treatments.description: Side-by-side Thai oil massages in our couples suite, followed by a herbal steam bath and a platter of local fruit and herbal teas.
treatments.cancellationPolicy: Cancel at least 4 hours in advance.

options.name: Airport Arrival Transfer
options.description: Chauffeur-driven transfer from BIA to Dambulla.

options.name: Airport Departure Transfer
options.description: Chauffeur-driven transfer from Dambulla to BIA.

experiences.name: Sigiriya Rock Fortress — Sunrise Climb
experiences.description: Beat the crowds with an early morning guided ascent of the iconic Sigiriya Lion Rock. Watch the Cultural Triangle light up as the sun rises.

experiences.name: Minneriya Elephant Gathering Safari
experiences.description: Witness hundreds of wild Asian elephants gathering at Minneriya Tank — one of the greatest wildlife spectacles on earth.

experiences.name: Dambulla Cave Temple Tour
experiences.description: Explore five cave temples filled with 150 Buddha statues and stunning 2,000-year-old murals. A UNESCO World Heritage Site just 1km away.

localPlaces.name: Dambulla Cave Temple (Golden Temple)
localPlaces.description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site and the largest cave temple complex in Asia, carved into a 160 m granite outcrop above the town. Five caves contain 153 statues of the Buddha and 2,100 sq m of intricate murals spanning over 22 centuries, from 1st century BC to the 18th century. The site has been a place of continuous pilgrimage for over two millennia.
localPlaces.travelTime: 3 min by car or 15 min walk
localPlaces.hotelNote: Visit before 09:00 or after 16:00 to avoid large tour groups. Modest dress is required — sarongs are available at the entrance. Remove shoes before entering the cave complex.

localPlaces.name: Sigiriya Lion Rock Fortress
localPlaces.description: Sri Lanka's most iconic sight — a 5th-century royal citadel perched atop a 200 m volcanic rock plug rising sheer from the jungle. Built by King Kashyapa (477–495 AD), the fortress features the famous Lion's Paw entrance gate, a network of gardens (the world's oldest surviving landscaped gardens), and extraordinary frescoes of heavenly maidens painted directly onto the rock face. A UNESCO World Heritage Site.
localPlaces.hotelNote: Our tour desk arranges early-morning guided ascents (05:30 departure) to beat the heat and tour buses. The climb takes 45–90 minutes. Good footwear and water are essential.

localPlaces.name: Pidurangala Rock
localPlaces.description: The local alternative to Sigiriya, Pidurangala is a 310 m monastic rock rising immediately north of Sigiriya Rock and provides the best aerial view of the Lion Rock — beloved by photographers. The 40-minute climb passes through the 5th-century Pidurangala Rajamaha Viharaya temple with a reclining rock Buddha, then scrambles across exposed boulders to the summit.
localPlaces.hotelNote: Sunrise from Pidurangala with Sigiriya glowing in the pink morning light is one of the best photographs you will ever take. Depart our hotel by 05:30. The hike requires basic bouldering — closed shoes essential.

localPlaces.name: Minneriya National Park — The Elephant Gathering
localPlaces.description: Between July and October, the annual 'Gathering' at the ancient Minneriya Tank draws up to 300–350 wild Asian elephants — the largest temporary concentration of Asian elephants on earth. As the dry season lowers the lake, herds from across the surrounding forests converge to graze on the fresh grass emerging from the receding shoreline. Outside gathering season, the 8,890-hectare park still offers excellent elephant sightings year-round.
localPlaces.entryFee: Approx. USD 25–35 per person (plus jeep hire)
localPlaces.hotelNote: The afternoon session (14:30 departure) is far better than morning for elephant sightings at the tank shore. Our tour desk arranges jeep safaris with naturalist guides — book the evening before.

localPlaces.name: Ancient City of Polonnaruwa
localPlaces.description: Sri Lanka's medieval capital (11th–13th century AD) and a UNESCO World Heritage Site — one of the best-preserved ancient cities in Asia. The 6 km² site encompasses a royal palace, perfectly proportioned moonstone thresholds, the magnificent Gal Vihara rock temple with its colossal carved Buddhas, and extensive Buddhist monasteries. The scale and condition of the ruins rival Angkor Wat.
localPlaces.entryFee: USD 30 per adult foreigner (combined with Polonnaruwa Museum)
localPlaces.hotelNote: Hire a bicycle at the entrance gate — it's the best way to explore the 6 km² site. Allow at least half a day. Combine with a sunset visit to Minneriya for a full Cultural Triangle day.

localPlaces.name: Nalanda Gedige
localPlaces.description: A unique and almost forgotten 8th-century architectural gem at the precise geographic centre of Sri Lanka. Nalanda Gedige is a stone shrine built entirely without mortar that blends Hindu (Shaivite) and Buddhist iconography in a style unique in the island — a physical symbol of religious coexistence. The site is largely unvisited by tourists, lending it a mysterious, atmospheric quality.
localPlaces.travelTime: 30 min by car (south on A9 toward Matale)
localPlaces.hotelNote: Combine the visit with a stop at Nalanda tank's birdwatching area. This is one of the Cultural Triangle's most rewarding hidden sites — almost no tour buses come here.
